decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective first step is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat dissipation away from the chip, staying within the allowed 2x multiplier bound.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity maximizes the allowed single-step improvement to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the hot spot temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation.
